Home

Inducing Reflexivity

Reflexivity is the examination of one’s own beliefs, judgments and practices. It’s necessary for our learning and agency. For example, a Sprint Retrospective in Scrum is a formal way to do it in a team context.

In Academia, it’s positively related to performance(source), so a manager may try to induce it out of their team by mandating it as an activity. But a forced retro is yet another useless meeting for an engineer.

Goal Setting

Structured Activity

Observable Error

Repetition

Curiosity
